title: Prescription: How many years does a creditor in Quebec have to...
summary: Determine the prescription period for initiating a breach of contract lawsuit in Quebec.
prompt: How many years does a creditor in Quebec have to initiate a breach of contract lawsuit?

options.text: Three years or less from the time the offense occurred

solution: In Quebec, the general rule for prescription (limitation period) for breach of contract is three years or less from the time the offense occurred. This means that a creditor must initiate a lawsuit within this timeframe; otherwise, the right to sue may be lost. Understanding this limitation is crucial for both creditors and debtors in managing their legal rights.
